arts.ttl: Lecce vs Fiorentina, for the three points
arts.sum: Lecce won their last home match in Serie A against Fiorentina while only once have they managed two consecutive home wins against the Tuscans in the competition: between 1989 (under Carlo Mazzone) and 1991 (under Zbigniew Boniek). In the last 13 Serie A meetings between Lecce and Fiorentina, the same result has never been repeated in consecutive games, with five victories for the Apulians (including a 3-2 win in their last encounter in the competition), four draws and four wins for the Tuscans.
Lecce will aim to move out of the dropzone when they face Fiorentina at the Stadio Via del Mare today. Lecce prevailed against Fiorentina the last time these two sides…

arts.ttl: Gotti welcomes back Injured players ahead of Fiorentina clash
arts.sum: Lecce manager Luca Gotti addressed the media in a press conference ahead of his team’s match against Fiorentina, providing updates on player fitness and tactical decisions. Regarding the returning Kaba, Gotti said: “After Udinese, he rejoined the squad. He’s fit but needs to regain the intensity required for Serie A. He’s a muscular midfielder with great verticality, and he can give us some additional solutions.” On Banda’s condition, the coach expressed optimism: “I’ll touch wood. After the Milan match, Banda stopped and did 12 days of rehabilitation only. arts.ttl: Serie A | Lecce v Fiorentina: Match Preview
arts.sum: The Stadio Via del Mare will host an intriguing Serie A clash on Sunday afternoon as struggling Lecce welcome an improving Fiorentina side. With contrasting recent form and ambitions, both teams will be eager to secure a positive result as domestic action resumes following the international break. Lecce find themselves in a precarious position near the foot of the Serie A table, sitting 17th with just 5 points from their opening 7 matches. Head coach Luca Gotti is under increasing pressure to turn things around after a five-game winless run in all competitions. arts.ttl: Palladino: Milan victory boosted Fiorentina’s momentum ahead of crucial month
arts.sum: Fiorentina manager Raffaele Palladino spoke to the club’s official channels two days before their clash with Lecce, expressing optimism about the team’s recent progress and the challenges ahead. “These have been days where we’ve maintained continuity in our work,” Palladino began. “They’ve been very useful. In today’s football, you play every three days, and being able to spend as much time as possible with the players is important. We’ve worked hard and sweated, and we already want to give continuity to our results in Lecce on Sunday.” The coach acknowledged the surge of enthusiasm following Fiorentina’s victory over AC Milan.
